pipeline_status.analyst: EXP-140 completed (WIN-010/WIN-068 defense neutralization); proposal-ISS-841-resolution.json processed
pipeline_status.curmudgeon: 4 pending reviews processed this run: SEC-overview-scorecard.c3, eval-guide-eg-verdicts.c1, KILLSHOT-GAIA-v2.c2, EXP-143-proposal.c2

internal_issues.description: WIN-010/WIN-068 defense neutralization: eclipse geometry defense. Defense accepted — eclipse shadow tracks apparent geometry, not physical altitude. n(r) cascade argument does not apply to eclipse magnetic predictions.

eg-falsifiability Condition 1 claims 4 WINs attempt dome derivation (WIN-020, WIN-044, WIN-057, WIN-058) but data shows only 2 (WIN-044, WIN-057). WIN-020 and WIN-058 tagged derives_from_dome=false. Prose contradicts data — exactly what we criticize the dome for.

KILLSHOT-GAIA stellar parallax table labeled 'Gaia observations' but Sirius, Vega, Betelgeuse, Deneb parallax values are from Hipparcos (van Leeuwen 2007), not Gaia DR3 (which cannot measure these bright stars).
KILLSHOT-GAIA: New Horizons parallax experiment cited as 'Throop et al. 2020' but was led by Tod Lauer, not Throop. Should be 'Lauer et al. 2020'.

exp_143_status: EXP-143 already marked no_op/integrated in expansion tracker (superseded_by_EXP-144). Curmudgeon BLOCKED review noted this correctly. No action required.

recommended_actions.action: ISS-907 (major): Fix derives_from_dome count mismatch in eg-falsifiability. Either update WIN-020/WIN-058 code_analysis tags OR correct text to say 'only 2 of N claims'. Requires editing generate-html.js template — human or analyst action needed.
ISS-908 (moderate): Fix KILLSHOT-GAIA table — either relabel header or replace 4 bright stars with Gaia-only fainter stars. Option B (replacement) is stronger. Requires updating sections.json part5.
Duplicate issue cleanup: 5 pairs of duplicate issues identified (ISS-892/902, 893/903, 899/904, 895/905, 896/906). Human should close the older set with status 'duplicate' to reduce tracker noise.
ISS-909 (minor): Fix Throop citation in KILLSHOT-GAIA to Lauer et al. 2020.
